title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers fetch-to-commit ingestion unit, checkpoints, and partial failure into a changed atomic feed ingestion unit of work. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of atomic feed ingestion unit of work with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.
Commit feed metadata, entries, revisions, validators, next schedule, and audit record together or preserve the prior admitted version.
Checkpoint only replay-safe boundaries and bind each checkpoint to source, validator, parser version, schema, and attempt identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Ingestion State Machine
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4697 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Ingestion State Machine.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Ingestion State Machine, keep Feed Go Ingestion State Machine connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
